australian_relation: ABC News public article recounting the Arthur Marrin hairy-man/Yahoo encounter tradition near Braidwood, New South Wales.
coordinate_evidence_note: ABC page names the Arthur Marrin creature account near the NSW town of Braidwood; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Braidwood locality result.
evidence_summary: ABC News reported that the story of Arthur Marrin and his encounter with a hairy man in 1893 is part of the folklore of the New South Wales Southern Highlands. Historian Graham Joyner wrote that reports from south-eastern Australia described an unidentified animal variously called the Yahoo, Australian ape or Australian gorilla, and named Marrin's Braidwood account as one of the best-known examples.
publication_or_organisation: 'Hairy man' encountered by Arthur Marrin 123 years ago still unidentified; ABC News
title: hairy man in Arthur Marrin Braidwood hairy man
